articles.title: Wildfires burn through the night in California county
articles.description: Aerial footage filmed on Monday (June 15) showed wildfires burning in Southern California’s Riverside County. The blaze, dubbed the “Shore Fire”, had grown to an estimated 2,053 acres as of midnight (0700 GMT on June 16), according to the California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection (Cal Fire). - REUTERS

articles.title: 79 baby green sea turtles released in Panglao
articles.description: TAGBILARAN CITY, Bohol — A total of 79 baby green sea turtles were successfully released into the sea on Monday night on Alona Beach in Panglao, marking another milestone in Bohol’s ongoing marine conservation efforts. The release was facilitated by the Department of Environment and Natural Resources–Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (DENR-Cenro) – Tagbilaran, together with the Provincial Intelligence and Public Safety Office and the Provincial Internal Peace and Public Relations Program. READ: Green sea turtle nesting place found, sealed off in BatanesThe hatchlings came from a clutch of more than 227 sea turtle eggs that were discovered at [...]... articles.title: Italian Prime Minister Georgia Meloni tells G7 leaders that she quit smoking one month ago
articles.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni told fellow G7 leaders on Tuesday she had quit smoking. While waiting for US President Donald Trump, French President Emmanuel Macron and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to arrive for a meeting, G7 leaders struck up a conversation about smoking.

articles.title: Kanlaon Volcano releases ash for the 20th time
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — Kanlaon Volcano in Negros Island emitted ash on Tuesday afternoon, marking its 20th time of doing so in just a day, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) said. articles.title: Thousands of people rattled after 6.7-magnitude earthquake shook Sulawesi Island causing damage and injuries
articles.description: A 6.7 magnitude earthquake shook part of central Indonesia's Sulawesi island Tuesday, injuring at least eight people, causing scattered damage and rattling residents of a city devastated by a quake and tsunami eight years ago.
